note_emb001.title: text-embedding-3-small chosen over ada-002
note_emb001.preview: We use text-embedding-3-small: 5x cheaper than ada-002 with 2% better recall on our QA benchmark.

note_emb002.title: pgvector HNSW index tuning baseline
note_emb002.preview: pgvector HNSW runs m=16, ef_construction=128, ef_search=100; recall@10 improved 0.91→0.96 with no measurable write-latency hit at current scale.

note_emb003.title: Embedding batch size limit — 2048 tokens per item
note_emb003.preview: text-embedding-3-small silently truncates inputs over 8191 tokens — chunk documents before embedding, not after.

note_emb004.title: Embedding reindex strategy — nightly incremental, full weekly
note_emb004.preview: We run nightly incremental reindexing (changed docs only) and a full reindex every Sunday 3am UTC to catch drift.

note_auth001.title: JWT token expiry — 15 min access, 7 day refresh
note_auth001.preview: Access tokens expire in 15 minutes; refresh tokens expire in 7 days — matched to legal's session compliance requirement.

note_auth002.title: Auth middleware order — rate limiter must run before JWT validation
note_auth002.preview: Rate limiter middleware must be registered before JWT validation — otherwise invalid tokens bypass rate limiting.

note_dep001.title: Deploy to Railway — Dockerfile + railway up
note_dep001.preview: Deployment target is Fly.io; ship with `fly deploy`.

note_db001.title: Switched from Pinecone to pgvector — cost + operational simplicity
note_db001.preview: Replaced Pinecone with pgvector: saves $340/month and fits our sub-500k vector corpus without managed scaling.

note_db002.title: Postgres connection pooling — PgBouncer in transaction mode
note_db002.preview: PgBouncer in transaction mode caps our Postgres connections at 20 regardless of API pod count.

note_db003.title: Database migration strategy — Alembic with zero-downtime column adds
note_db003.preview: Always add nullable columns first, backfill in a separate migration, then add NOT NULL constraint.

note_rl8k2m.title: Rate limiting — 60 req/min per API key via Redis token bucket
note_rl8k2m.preview: 60 req/min per API key via Redis token bucket, burst 10, 429 + Retry-After on limit.

note_bil001.title: Stripe chosen for billing — SOC 2 / PCI offload
note_bil001.preview: Stripe handles billing: chosen to inherit SOC 2/PCI compliance instead of spending ~6 months building our own payment infra.

note_hnsw42.title: Missing HNSW index caused 400ms vector queries — always use vector_cosine_ops
note_hnsw42.preview: Vector similarity queries ran at 400ms because the embedding column had only a btree index; adding an HNSW index dropped latency to 8ms.

note_meta001.title: What Unscatter is and why it exists
note_meta001.preview: Unscatter is a local-first AI tool that turns voice, text, and file dumps into a structured, queryable company knowledge base.

note_meta002.title: How to capture knowledge in Unscatter
note_meta002.preview: Three ways to capture: menu bar voice (ambient), web UI capture modal (voice/text/file), or direct text dump.

note_meta003.title: How to query and export knowledge from Unscatter
note_meta003.preview: Browse the wiki, ask the chatbot (it cites sources), explore the graph with 4 overlays, or export topics as Claude Skills.

note_eml001.title: Resend chosen over SendGrid for transactional email
note_eml001.preview: We use Resend (not SendGrid) for transactional email: cleaner API, usage-based pricing with no monthly minimum, first-class React Email support.

note_pgto30.title: Postgres statement_timeout — 30s primary, 5m replica for reports
note_pgto30.preview: Primary Postgres enforces a 30s statement_timeout; long-running reports run on a separate replica with a 5-minute timeout.

note_cma001.title: Claude Managed Agents — what it is and core primitives
note_cma001.preview: Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents (public beta, Apr 8 2026) offers hosted infra for running Claude as an autonomous agent — no DIY agent loop.

note_cma002.title: Claude Managed Agents pricing — $0.08/session-hour + tokens
note_cma002.preview: Standard Claude token rates plus $0.08 per active session-hour; a 24/7 agent runs ~$58/month in runtime before tokens.

note_cma003.title: Managed Agents lock-in risk — Claude-only, key features gated
note_cma003.preview: Managed Agents runs Claude only; the most compelling features (multi-agent, self-eval) are gated research previews — real vendor lock-in risk.
